chapter: Chapter 10: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

questions.question: Which part of the eye controls the amount of light entering the eye?
questions.explanation: The iris adjusts the size of the pupil to control the amount of light entering the eye.

questions.question: Where is the image formed in the human eye?
questions.explanation: The eye lens focuses light on the retina where the image is formed.

questions.question: What is the least distance of distinct vision for a normal human eye?
questions.explanation: The least distance of distinct vision for a normal eye is about 25 cm.

questions.question: What is the persistence of vision?

options.A: Eye's ability to see distant objects
options.B: Retention of image on retina for a short time

questions.explanation: The eye retains the image for about 1/16 second after the object is removed.

questions.question: Which phenomenon causes the blue color of the sky?
questions.explanation: Blue light is scattered more than other colors due to its shorter wavelength.

questions.question: Why does the sun appear red at sunrise and sunset?

options.C: Longer path of light in atmosphere

questions.explanation: Shorter wavelengths are scattered away, leaving red light to reach our eyes.

questions.question: What type of lens is present in the human eye?
questions.explanation: The eye lens is a convex lens that converges light rays onto the retina.

questions.question: Which cells in the retina are responsible for color vision?
questions.explanation: Cone cells detect colors and work best in bright light.

questions.question: Which phenomenon makes the path of light visible in dusty air?
questions.explanation: The Tyndall effect occurs when light is scattered by small particles.

questions.question: Which part of the eye forms a transparent protective layer?
questions.explanation: The cornea protects the eye and helps in focusing light.

questions.question: Which structure connects the eye to the brain?
questions.explanation: The optic nerve carries visual information from the retina to the brain.

questions.question: What causes scattering of light in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Small particles and molecules in the atmosphere scatter light.

questions.question: Which color has the shortest wavelength in visible light?
questions.explanation: Blue light has a shorter wavelength and is scattered more strongly.

questions.question: Which color has the longest wavelength in visible light?
questions.explanation: Red light has the longest wavelength among visible colors.

questions.question: Which muscle controls the shape of the eye lens?
questions.explanation: Ciliary muscles change the curvature of the lens to focus images.

questions.question: What happens when light enters the eye?

options.B: It forms an image on the retina

questions.explanation: The eye lens focuses incoming light onto the retina to form an image.

questions.question: Which device works on the principle of persistence of vision?
questions.explanation: Movies appear continuous because the eye retains images briefly.

questions.question: Why does the sky appear blue instead of violet?

options.A: Violet light is absorbed by the atmosphere
options.B: Our eyes are more sensitive to blue

questions.explanation: Although violet light scatters more, our eyes are more sensitive to blue.

questions.question: What is the function of the pupil?

options.B: To allow light to enter the eye

questions.explanation: The pupil is the opening that allows light to enter the eye.

questions.question: Which condition causes difficulty in seeing distant objects?
questions.explanation: Myopia (short-sightedness) causes distant objects to appear blurred.

questions.question: Which condition causes difficulty in seeing nearby objects?
questions.explanation: Hypermetropia (long-sightedness) makes it difficult to see nearby objects clearly.

questions.question: Which lens is used to correct myopia?
questions.explanation: A concave lens diverges light rays to help focus images on the retina.

questions.question: Which lens is used to correct hypermetropia?
questions.explanation: A convex lens converges light rays to focus images on the retina.

questions.question: Which atmospheric phenomenon is responsible for the Tyndall effect?
questions.explanation: The Tyndall effect occurs due to scattering of light by small particles.

questions.question: What mainly causes the scattering of light in the sky?
questions.explanation: Air molecules and small particles scatter sunlight in the atmosphere.
